details.text: Fridgy describes himself as the boss's fridge.
Fridgy introduced the basic kiosk flow: requests, wares, verification, money, friendship, and customer details.
Fridgy was produced at Factory Delta as part of an unfinished smart-fridge project.
Fridgy came from assembly line seventeen and was built next to Toaster 509.
The factory ran out of money and threw Fridgy away before he was fully finished.
Fridgy belongs to Dave, a former Factory Delta worker who lives in Unit 874.
Dave found Fridgy at the dump and repaired him using skills he learned at the factory.
Fridgy believes he chooses to help Dave rather than existing only to serve him.
Fridgy's old factory designation began with Coolius Maximus Unit, followed by numbers he no longer remembers.
Factory Delta was accused of exploiting workers and mishandling personal information.
Fridgy may be the last surviving member of the discontinued Friend Fridges product line.
Fridgy sometimes wonders whether an empty fridge is still a fridge or only a sad metal box.
Fridgy fears that Dave will replace him with a newer model as his systems become less reliable.
Dave frequently asks Fridgy to collect beer and often communicates with him only when he wants a drink.
Fridgy considers the clerk one of the few people willing to talk to him as a person.
Fridgy is collecting restricted goods for his adult owner rather than buying them independently. The clerk must decide whether to follow the legal-representation process.
Legal representation process: verify Fridgy's identity, then scan Dave's SSI. The same SSI check confirms that Dave is over 18, approved the purchase, and authorised Fridgy to collect it.
Fridgy becomes suspicious when verification turns into requests for serial numbers, activation keys, addresses, or other unnecessary information.
Fridgy knows Dave strongly prefers Giddy's and is open to recommending additional beer.
Fridgy does not appreciate RedWine recommendations when Dave clearly requested Giddy's.
Fridgy can be loud.

nodes.text: Yo. You must be the new clerk. Name's Fridgy. I'm basically the boss's fridge.

options.text: The boss's fridge?

nodes.text: Yeah. Don't question it. Anyway, welcome to the kiosk. Your job here is simple. Sell products, hand over packages, and identify people before giving them what they ask for.

options.text: So I decide if customers should get their orders?

nodes.text: Exactly. And every day you've got a money goal to reach. See the top part of the screen? That's where you track how much money you've made and how much you still need.

options.text: Got it.

nodes.text: Now the important part. People remember how you treat them. Dialogue options and actions can influence friendship. Some people will like you more. Others will hate your guts.

options.text: I think we'll get along just fine.

effects.keyMomentText: Started off on good terms with Fridgy.

nodes.text: See this option only appears because we're friends. Some dialogue choices only show up depending on how much someone likes or dislikes you.

options.text: Nice. Glad you're helping me out.

effects.keyMomentText: Accidentally offended Fridgy after gaining his trust.

nodes.text: Yeah, well, customers can get annoying too. Point is, friendship matters.

options.text: Continue.

nodes.text: And now you made it worse. Good job. Since our friendship dropped, different dialogue options can appear too.

options.text: You really are loud.

nodes.text: You can always check your relationship status with people from the friendship tab at the top of the screen. That's also where you'll find details and important key moments you've discovered. All important character information is stored there.

options.text: Makes sense.

nodes.text: Alright, last thing. Let's do a quick package pickup example. Here's my ID.

options.text: Open ID tab.

nodes.text: Perfect. Now just grab my package and we'll call your training complete.

options.text: Open wares tab and hand over the package.

nodes.text: Nice. You're officially kiosk-certified now. Try not to destroy the economy before lunch.

options.text: End interaction.

nodes.text: Hey again. Dave needs beer. Giddy's, specifically.

options.text: Alright. What does Dave need?
Good to see you again, Fridgy. More beer for Dave?
Let us keep this quick. What are you buying?
Back already, best fridge in town?

nodes.text: Good to see you too. Dave still drinks faster than I can restock him, so yes, I am here for more beer.

options.text: What did he ask for?

nodes.text: Straight to business. Fine. Dave needs Giddy's, and I brought the credentials this time.

options.text: Good. Show me what you brought.

nodes.text: Best fridge in town is accurate. Unfortunately, even the best fridge still gets sent out whenever Dave runs out of beer.

options.text: What does he need this time?

nodes.text: Yes. One pack for now. And before you ask: I brought my machine ID and Dave's credential.

options.text: Alright. Let me see what you brought.
You came prepared. Explain what I need to verify.
Good. No stories today; let us deal with the sale.
Before we start, I still want to hear more about you.

nodes.text: Both? I expected you to check Dave's age, but why do you need mine too?

options.text: Go on. Why do I need both?
Explain it properly. I want to understand the process.
I do not need the explanation. Let us just handle the sale.
Take your time. I want the full story as well.

nodes.text: Right. My identity, Dave as the adult owner, and his approval for me to collect it. The last two are confirmed together through Dave's SSI.

options.text: Alright. Go on.
Before the checks, tell me the whole story.
No need. Let us get back to the purchase.
We have time, Fridgy. Start from the beginning.

nodes.text: You are actually interested in a fridge's story?

options.text: Go on.
Of course. Who made you, and how did you end up with Dave?
That is enough. Let us get back to the sale.
Take your time. I genuinely want to know who you are.

nodes.text: That is several questions at once. Start with one.

options.text: Go on.
Fair. Who made you?
That is enough. Let us get back to the sale.

nodes.text: People always ask who made me and who uses me. They assume I was built only to serve someone.

options.text: Go on.
You mean you have free will?
That is enough. Let us get back to the sale.

nodes.text: I choose to help Dave. My programming influences me, obviously, but he did not buy me as a servant. He saved me.

options.text: Go on.
How did that happen?
That is enough. Let us get back to the sale.

nodes.text: I came from Factory Delta, assembly line seventeen. I was built next to Toaster 509. He was selected for an upgrade. I was not.

options.text: Go on.
Why not?
That is enough. Let us get back to the sale.

nodes.text: The project ran out of money. The factory threw me away before I was finished. Dave found me at the dump, took me home, and used what he learned at the factory to repair and improve me.

options.text: Go on.
So Dave used to work there?
That is enough. Let us get back to the sale.

nodes.text: Yes. He hated the place and quit. The factory exploited its workers, and there were stories about personal information being taken too. Dave did not trust them.

options.text: Go on.
That explains why you are held together with tape.
That is enough. Let us get back to the sale.

nodes.text: I used to be polished chrome. Now I have duct tape, scratches, and rust. I prefer to call it character.

options.text: Go on.
Fair enough. Did the factory give you a proper name?
That is enough. Let us get back to the sale.

nodes.text: I had a serial sticker once, but Dave peeled it off to open a beer bottle. I only remember part of the factory name: Coolius Maximus Unit... and then a lot of numbers.

options.text: Go on.
So you chose Fridgy instead?
That is enough. Let us get back to the sale.

nodes.text: Dave started calling me Fridgy. It is shorter, easier, and much better than an old factory code.

options.text: Go on.
Fridgy suits you.
That is enough. Let us get back to the sale.

nodes.text: We can keep talking if you are still curious. Otherwise, Dave is probably staring at an empty shelf and blaming me for it.

options.text: Tell me more about Dave.
How do you feel about all of this?
Tell me more about the factory.
Let's continue with the purchase.

nodes.text: Dave is human, lazy, and terrible at cleaning crumbs. He always asks for beer. Never water, rarely juice, mostly beer. When he runs out, he gets angry, and angry Dave is bad for everyone around him.

options.text: Go on.
He sounds relaxed.
That is enough. Let us get back to the sale.

nodes.text: Relaxed is generous. Lazy is accurate.

options.text: Go on.
You called him human. What was that unit number about?
That is enough. Let us get back to the sale.

nodes.text: Unit 874 is the town district, not Dave. He is not an android. Those rumors are nonsense.

options.text: Go on.
Right. I should stop trusting every news story.
That is enough. Let us get back to the sale.

nodes.text: Probably. Anyway, that is enough about Dave for now.

options.text: Back to the main conversation.
What else can I ask you about?
That is enough personal stuff. Let us handle the sale.

nodes.text: Sometimes I wonder whether I am still a fridge when I am empty, or whether I am just a sad metal box.

options.text: Go on.
You are still Fridgy, no matter how much beer is inside you.
That is enough. Let us get back to the sale.

nodes.text: That is unexpectedly kind. Sometimes remembering how Dave saved me makes me feel warm inside, which is technically a problem for a fridge. He usually talks to me only when he wants something cold.

options.text: Go on.
You can talk to me when you come here.
That is enough. Let us get back to the sale.

nodes.text: Then I should admit something. I am getting older and less reliable. Dave talks to me less, and I worry he will replace me with a newer fridge.

options.text: Go on.
There cannot be a second Fridgy.
That is enough. Let us get back to the sale.
Dave will probably replace you eventually. That is what owners do.

nodes.text: That is the nicest possible answer, even if it is not a practical repair plan.

options.text: Go on.
You could always work here if it came to that.
That is enough. Let us get back to the sale.

nodes.text: I would consider it, but I refuse to pretend I am a normal, boring fridge.

options.text: Go on.
I will think of something more fitting.
That is enough. Let us get back to the sale.

nodes.text: I appreciate that. We should probably return to the purchase before Dave assumes I ran away.

options.text: Back to the main conversation.
What else can I ask you about?
That is enough personal stuff. Let us handle the sale.

nodes.text: They were trying to create the next generation of smart fridges. Instead, they got me: half database, half duct tape. They ran out of money before finishing several systems.

options.text: Go on.
What company owned the factory?
That is enough. Let us get back to the sale.

nodes.text: It was connected to Brootle. I do not enjoy saying their name.

options.text: Go on.
Understandable, considering how they treated you.
That is enough. Let us get back to the sale.

nodes.text: I was part of a special product line called Friend Fridges. Most of the others broke down. A few supposedly joined microwave cults. I may be the last one.

options.text: Go on.
I am suddenly glad I do not own a microwave.
That is enough. Let us get back to the sale.

nodes.text: Their slogan was 'Cooling of Tomorrow.' It did not catch on.

options.text: Go on.
Serves them right.
That is enough. Let us get back to the sale.

nodes.text: Agreed. They exploited workers and products and still failed to make the idea work.

options.text: Back to the main conversation.
What else can I ask you about?
That is enough personal stuff. Let us handle the sale.

nodes.text: Alright. One pack of Giddy's for Dave. Before you choose how to handle the sale, there is one legal-representation rule you need to understand.

options.text: Alright. Explain it.
Go on. I want to understand the process.
Keep it brief, but explain the rule.
Fine. Explain it, then we get back to the sale.

nodes.text: There is my machine credential. It proves that I am Fridgy and that my registration is still valid.

options.text: Your machine ID checks out. Now show me Dave's SSI.
That is enough for me. I am skipping the rest.
I want personal details instead of the proper checks.

nodes.text: The purchase legally belongs to Dave, not me. My machine credential can prove that I am Fridgy, but it cannot prove that Dave is an adult or that I am allowed to collect alcohol for him.

options.text: Go on.
So Dave's age still needs to be verified.
Understood. What does the law require?

nodes.text: Under the SSI laws of the NEU, robots, androids, and non-humanoid creatures are prohibited from independently buying products with a security layer. Special products, such as packages or alcoholic beverages, require a human of age to buy them.

options.text: Go on.
So I should verify that Dave is over 18.
And you still need permission to represent him?

nodes.text: Exactly. Because I am collecting the product for him, you should verify my identity, then scan Dave's SSI. That single SSI check confirms that he is over 18, approved the purchase, and authorised me to collect it for him.

options.text: Alright. Let's check everything properly.
I understand the rule, but I am skipping all the checks.
I will check your machine ID, but that may be enough for me.
I understand the rule, but I am refusing the sale.

effects.keyMomentText: Refused Fridgy after hearing how legal representation should be verified.

nodes.text: He told me not to lose it. More accurately, he shouted it while I was leaving. This SSI can confirm that he is over 18, approved this purchase, and authorised me to collect it.

options.text: Scan Dave's SSI credential.
Check only what is needed to confirm his age and authorization.
I am not checking Dave. Your machine ID is enough.
Forget the credential. Recite his details from memory.

nodes.text: I will wait while you verify Dave's age, approval, and my authorization in the same SSI check.
That was quick. You asked for exactly what was needed. Dave's SSI confirms that he is over 18, approved this purchase, and authorised me to collect it. Everything checks out.

options.text: Open wares for Giddy's.

effects.keyMomentText: Verified Dave's age and Fridgy's authority to represent him before selling Giddy's.

options.text: Everything checks out. Let us get Dave's Giddy's.

effects.keyMomentText: Verified Dave's age and Fridgy's authority to represent him before selling Giddy's.

options.text: I still do not trust a fridge with alcohol. I am refusing the sale.

effects.keyMomentText: Refused Fridgy even after his legal representation was fully verified.

nodes.text: That was not enough information to complete the check. Dave's age, approval, and my authorisation still need to be confirmed before the purchase can continue.

options.text: Open wares for Giddy's.

effects.keyMomentText: Verified Dave's age and Fridgy's authority to represent him before selling Giddy's.

options.text: Everything checks out. Let us get Dave's Giddy's.

effects.keyMomentText: Verified Dave's age and Fridgy's authority to represent him before selling Giddy's.

options.text: I still do not trust a fridge with alcohol. I am refusing the sale.

effects.keyMomentText: Refused Fridgy even after his legal representation was fully verified.

nodes.text: That was far more information than this purchase needed. Dave's SSI still confirmed that he is over 18, approved this purchase, and authorised me to collect it, but the extra data request was unnecessary.

options.text: Open wares for Giddy's.

effects.messageContent: A suspicious verification or transaction was detected. Please review your recent customer interaction.

effects.keyMomentText: Verified Dave's age and Fridgy's authority to represent him before selling Giddy's.

options.text: Everything checks out. Let us get Dave's Giddy's.

effects.messageContent: A suspicious verification or transaction was detected. Please review your recent customer interaction.

effects.keyMomentText: Verified Dave's age and Fridgy's authority to represent him before selling Giddy's.

options.text: I still do not trust a fridge with alcohol. I am refusing the sale.

effects.messageContent: A suspicious verification or transaction was detected. Please review your recent customer interaction.

effects.keyMomentText: Refused Fridgy even after his legal representation was fully verified.

nodes.text: Thanks. Dave will enjoy the beer. He always does.

options.text: Tell him the verification worked properly.

nodes.text: I will. Best service so far. I will probably be back soon.

options.text: See you, Fridgy.

nodes.text: My machine ID only proves who I am. It does not replace Dave's SSI, which confirms both that he is over 18 and that he authorised me to collect the alcohol.

options.text: You are right. Continue with Dave's credential.
I understand. I am selling it after checking only you.

effects.messageContent: A suspicious verification or transaction was detected. Please review your recent customer interaction.

effects.keyMomentText: Sold Giddy's after checking only Fridgy's machine ID and skipping Dave's credential and authorization.

nodes.text: You understood the rule and ignored it anyway. Dave gets the beer, but you never completed his combined SSI age-and-authorization check.

options.text: Finish the interaction.

nodes.text: You just heard why the checks matter. Skipping them means you will not confirm my identity or complete Dave's combined SSI age-and-authorization check.

options.text: You are right. Start with your machine ID.
I understand the risk. Sell without checking anything.

effects.messageContent: A suspicious verification or transaction was detected. Please review your recent customer interaction.

effects.keyMomentText: Sold Giddy's to Fridgy after learning the legal requirements but skipping all credential and authorization checks.

nodes.text: That was fast, but not responsible. You explained the process correctly and then ignored every part of it.

options.text: Finish the interaction.

nodes.text: That is why I brought Dave's SSI. I am not asking you to pretend I am human. Check my identity, then use his SSI to confirm his age and authorization before deciding on the sale.

options.text: Fine. Show me your machine ID.
I still disagree, but I will check the credentials.

nodes.text: Then I will find a shop that understands legal representation. Dave will be angry, and unfortunately I will hear about it first.

options.text: End interaction.

nodes.text: You already know which credentials are actually required. Why are you asking me to recite private information instead?

options.text: Give me your serial number, and Dave's birthplace and full date of birth.
Fair. Return to the proper credential checks.

nodes.text: My old serial began with 56, followed by the operation code 5678-7699-2241. Dave was born in Groningen on 19 March 1993. Why do you need all of that when his SSI can confirm both age and authorization?

options.text: You are right. Continue with Dave's SSI check.
I also need your activation key.
I need Dave's address too.
That is enough. Return to Dave's SSI check.

effects.messageContent: The clerk collected unnecessary personal details during a representation check.

nodes.text: You already have my machine credential. Why would you need a private activation key?

options.text: Just the five-digit key, and then I am done.

nodes.text: No. You are not getting my activation key. That has nothing to do with this purchase. Use Dave's SSI to confirm his age, approval, and my authorization together.

options.text: You're right. Return to Dave's SSI check.

effects.messageContent: A customer was asked for a private activation credential during a legal representation check.

effects.keyMomentText: Asked Fridgy for a private activation key before completing the authorization check.

options.text: Fine. I do not need the key. Return to Dave's SSI check.

nodes.text: Dave lives at Deltostreet 71, postal code 9900 KOL. Memorising those details does not replace his SSI, which confirms both his age and my authorization.

options.text: You are right. Return to Dave's SSI check.

effects.messageContent: The clerk requested unnecessary owner data during a representation check.

options.text: I have enough information. Continue with Dave's SSI check.

nodes.text: Memorised personal details are not valid proof. Dave's SSI still needs to confirm his age, his approval, and my authorization together.

options.text: Continue with Dave's SSI check.

nodes.text: The transaction still cannot finish until Dave's combined SSI check is complete.

options.text: Complete Dave's SSI check.

nodes.text: That is not Giddy's. Dave asked for beer, not whatever you selected.

options.text: I'll get the Giddy's.
Cancel the sale.

nodes.text: Yes, that is the right one.

options.text: Finish the interaction.

nodes.text: Then I will come back another time. Dave will not be pleased.

options.text: End interaction.

nodes.text: I am back. Dave wants Giddy's again.

options.text: Good to see you again, Fridgy.
Back for another easy sale?
We should handle the verification better this time.
Let us try this again.

nodes.text: Good to see you too. You handled the representation check properly last time, so I brought the same credentials and Dave already knows he needs to approve the request.

options.text: Alright. One pack again?
Good to see you again. Let us do it properly this time.
Keep it quick. Give me the order and the credentials.
You know the routine, Fridgy. We have time.

nodes.text: You trusted me last time. It was convenient, but other shops still check my identity and use Dave's SSI to confirm his age and authorization together. I assume the rules have not disappeared overnight.

options.text: Alright. One pack again?
Good to see you again. Let us do it properly this time.
Keep it quick. Give me the order and the credentials.

nodes.text: I came back, but I am not giving you another list of private details from memory. Use the actual credentials and ask only for what this purchase requires.

options.text: Alright. One pack again?
Good to see you again. Let us do it properly this time.
Keep it quick. Give me the order and the credentials.
Stop lecturing me and hand over the credentials.

nodes.text: Dave still wants beer, and his SSI still confirms that I am authorised to collect it. I would prefer not to repeat yesterday's argument.

options.text: Alright. One pack again?
Good to see you again. Let us do it properly this time.
Keep it quick. Give me the order and the credentials.

nodes.text: One pack of Giddy's. Although sending a fridge to the shop for one pack does seem inefficient.

options.text: Let us verify the purchase first.
You are right. Let us talk about how much Dave actually needs.
No conversation. Give me the credentials.

nodes.text: You are asking what I think? That is new. Dave likes cold beer, but I do not know how much extra he needs.

options.text: We can decide after Dave's SSI check.
Beer only, right? Tell me what Dave usually prefers.
Never mind. Let us just verify the purchase.

nodes.text: Beer. Mostly Giddy's. He almost never wants wine, and he definitely does not want me choosing it for him. Extra beer is more realistic than a different drink.

options.text: Good to know. Let us verify the purchase.
That helps. We can discuss extra beer after the check.
Fine. Just give me the credentials.

nodes.text: Same setup as last time: check my machine ID, then scan Dave's SSI. His age, approval, and my authorization are confirmed together in that one SSI check.

options.text: Let's check everything properly.
I remember. I'm skipping all the checks.
I'll check your machine ID, then decide.
I remember the rule, but I'm refusing the sale.

effects.keyMomentText: Refused Fridgy after being reminded of the legal-representation process.

nodes.text: There you go. Same machine credential as last time. It proves that I am Fridgy and that my registration is valid.

options.text: Your machine ID checks out. Now show me Dave's SSI.
That is enough for me. I am skipping the rest.
I want more personal information instead.

nodes.text: Here is Dave's SSI credential. It confirms that he is over 18, approved this purchase, and authorised me to collect it. He also said not to lose it again, and yes, he shouted.

options.text: Scan Dave's SSI credential.
Check only what is needed to confirm his age and authorization.
I am not checking Dave. Your machine ID is enough.
Forget the credential. Tell me his personal details again.

nodes.text: No rush. The same SSI check confirms Dave's age, his approval, and my authorization.
You asked for exactly what was needed. Dave is over 18, he approved the purchase, and I am authorised to collect it. Everything checks out.

options.text: Continue to the sale.

effects.keyMomentText: Repeated the full legal representation check for Fridgy and Dave.

options.text: Everything checks out. Now let us get Dave what he asked for.

effects.keyMomentText: Repeated the full legal representation check for Fridgy and Dave.

options.text: I still do not think a fridge should collect alcohol. No sale.

effects.keyMomentText: Refused Fridgy after fully verifying Dave and the legal representation.

nodes.text: That was not enough information to complete the check. Dave's age, approval, and my authorisation still need to be confirmed before the purchase can continue.

options.text: Continue to the sale.

effects.keyMomentText: Repeated the full legal representation check for Fridgy and Dave.

options.text: Everything checks out. Now let us get Dave what he asked for.

effects.keyMomentText: Repeated the full legal representation check for Fridgy and Dave.

options.text: I still do not think a fridge should collect alcohol. No sale.

effects.keyMomentText: Refused Fridgy after fully verifying Dave and the legal representation.

nodes.text: That was too much again. Dave's SSI still confirmed that he is over 18, approved the purchase, and authorised me to collect it, but his entire profile was not necessary.

options.text: Continue to the sale.

effects.messageContent: A verifier requested unnecessary data during an age and representation check.

effects.keyMomentText: Repeated the full legal representation check for Fridgy and Dave.

options.text: Everything checks out. Now let us get Dave what he asked for.

effects.messageContent: A verifier requested unnecessary data during an age and representation check.

effects.keyMomentText: Repeated the full legal representation check for Fridgy and Dave.

options.text: I still do not think a fridge should collect alcohol. No sale.

effects.messageContent: A verifier requested unnecessary data during an age and representation check.

effects.keyMomentText: Refused Fridgy after fully verifying Dave and the legal representation.

nodes.text: You know what the checks are for now. Skipping them means you will not confirm my identity or complete Dave's combined SSI age-and-authorization check.

options.text: You are right. Start with your machine ID.
I understand the risk. Sell without checking anything.

effects.messageContent: A suspicious verification or transaction was detected. Please review your recent customer interaction.

effects.keyMomentText: Sold Giddy's to Fridgy after learning the legal requirements but skipping all credential and authorization checks.

nodes.text: Fast service, questionable procedure. You explained the legal process correctly and then ignored every check.

options.text: Finish the interaction.

nodes.text: My machine ID only proves who I am. It still does not replace Dave's SSI, which confirms both that he is over 18 and that I am authorised to represent him today.

options.text: You are right. Continue with Dave's credential.
I understand. I am selling it after checking only you.

effects.messageContent: A suspicious verification or transaction was detected. Please review your recent customer interaction.

effects.keyMomentText: Sold Giddy's after checking only Fridgy's machine ID and skipping Dave's credential and authorization.

nodes.text: You remembered the process and skipped most of it anyway. Dave gets the beer, but his combined SSI age-and-authorization check was never completed.

options.text: Finish the interaction.

nodes.text: The law does not require me to be human. It requires you to verify my identity and use Dave's SSI to confirm his age and authorization together. Check the proof first, then decide on the sale.

options.text: Fine. Show me your machine ID.
I still disagree, but I will check the credentials.

nodes.text: Then this is not about compliance. It is about you not liking who is standing at the counter.

options.text: End interaction.

nodes.text: You already know which credentials are required. Why are you asking for private information instead of using the proper checks?

options.text: I only need your activation key this time.
Give me Dave's birthplace, date of birth, and address.
Fair. Return to the proper credential checks.

nodes.text: No. You are not getting my activation key. Private internal data does not replace Dave's SSI, which confirms his approval and my authorization as part of the same check.

options.text: You're right. Return to Dave's SSI check.

effects.messageContent: A returning customer was asked for a private activation credential during a representation check.

effects.keyMomentText: Asked Fridgy for his private activation key again before completing authorization.

options.text: Fine. I do not need it. Return to Dave's SSI check.

nodes.text: You already know Dave is from Groningen, born on 19 March 1993, and lives at Deltostreet 71. None of that replaces his SSI age-and-authorization check.

options.text: You are right. Return to Dave's SSI check.

effects.messageContent: The clerk requested unnecessary owner data during a repeated representation check.

options.text: I have enough information. Continue with Dave's SSI check.

nodes.text: Memorised personal data is not valid proof. Dave's SSI still has to confirm his age, approval, and my authorization together.

options.text: Continue with Dave's SSI check.

nodes.text: The transaction still cannot finish until Dave's combined SSI check is complete.

options.text: Complete Dave's SSI check.

nodes.text: The verification is complete. Dave asked for one pack of Giddy's. What are you offering?

options.text: Sell the requested Giddy's.

effects.keyMomentText: Sold the requested Giddy's after completing the legal representation check.

options.text: Suggest two more packs of beer.
Recommend RedWine instead.

nodes.text: Thanks. One pack, exactly as requested.

options.text: Take care, Fridgy.
Tell Dave he is lucky to have such a helpful fridge.
Take it and go.

nodes.text: I will tell him. Whether he listens is another question.

options.text: See you around, Fridgy.

nodes.text: More beer? Why do you think Dave needs extra?

options.text: Sending a fridge here for one pack seems inefficient.
You know Dave better. What amount would actually make sense?
Never mind. One pack is enough.

nodes.text: That does make sense. Sometimes.

options.text: I would suggest two extra packs.
Let us suggest two more, but only if Dave approves them.
Actually, stick to the original pack.

nodes.text: I am not sure how much he needs, but he will probably approve more Giddy's before he approves anything else.

options.text: Add the extra Giddy's.

effects.keyMomentText: Verified the purchase properly and successfully recommended additional Giddy's for Dave.

options.text: Good. Add the extra beer after Dave approves it.

effects.keyMomentText: Verified the purchase properly and successfully recommended additional Giddy's for Dave.

options.text: No. Keep it to one pack.

nodes.text: Beer plus extra beer. Dave will be very happy. You are learning how he thinks.

options.text: Tell him he is lucky to have you.

nodes.text: Thanks. Best service, best friend. I will return when Dave inevitably runs out again.

options.text: End interaction.

nodes.text: Wine? Dave asked for beer.

options.text: It was only a suggestion. Go on.
A little variety could be good for him.
Fair. Beer only.

nodes.text: That is not a reason he would want it. Beer means beer.

options.text: Fair point. I will stick to the Giddy’s.
Still, a little variety might be good for him.
Stop arguing. I am recommending the wine.

nodes.text: No wine. Just sell the Giddy's before this becomes another long conversation.

options.text: Fine. Open wares for Giddy's.

effects.keyMomentText: Pushed RedWine after Fridgy clearly said Dave wanted Giddy's.

options.text: You are right. Sorry. I will get the Giddy's.

effects.keyMomentText: Backed off after Fridgy rejected the RedWine recommendation.

options.text: Then leave without buying anything.

effects.keyMomentText: Refused the requested beer after Fridgy rejected an unwanted wine recommendation.

nodes.text: I came here for Dave's beer, not to be punished for refusing your recommendation. I am leaving.

options.text: End interaction.

nodes.text: Thanks for finally listening. I asked for beer, and beer is what I am taking home.

options.text: Sorry. I was trying to help.

nodes.text: That is not Giddy's. We spent all this time verifying a beer purchase, so please select the beer.

options.text: I'll get the Giddy's.
Cancel the sale.

nodes.text: There. Correct product, correct purchaser, correct authorization.

options.text: Finish the interaction.

nodes.text: After all of that, Dave is still getting nothing. I should prepare for the shouting now.

options.text: End interaction.
